insight: For Türkiye, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have been a significant factor in shifting the team's momentum, with a positive change in momentum observed in both breaks, averaging an increase of 0.100 minutes per minute. This suggests that the breaks have allowed Türkiye to regroup and come back stronger, potentially catching their opponents off guard. The data also indicates that when Türkiye was not pressing before a break, they tended to gain more momentum afterwards, with an average increase of 0.200 minutes per minute, implying that the team is able to capitalize on the pause to refocus and launch a stronger attack. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to have a rejuvenating effect on Türkiye, enabling them to regain their footing and push forward with renewed energy.
